summary: Woodward 给 Gemini 加了个第二大脑：Notebooks；Anthropic 则推出了 Managed Agents，把 Claude 从 prompt 直接推到 production。Rauch 把 web 叫作 AI 的原生 OS，Levie、Masad 和 Shipper 也都押注：以后干活的是 agents，不是人。

builderInsights.title: Josh Woodward
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: Gemini 通过 Notebooks 多了个第二大脑
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: Gemini 刚加了 Notebooks，把 NotebookLM 的一些最好用的功能直接搬进了 app。现在你可以免费上传最多 100 个 sources，整理 chats，还能让 sources、chats 和 emojis 保持同步。Google 也在把这个功能从 Ultra 逐步推到 Free。

tweets.text:
Most Al chatbots give you basic "projects." Gemini just built you a second brain. 🧠

Introducing Notebooks: some of the magic from @NotebookLM, integrated directly into @GeminiApp.

Here's what changes for you today:

📚 Upload 100 sources for free

📂 Organize your chats - the wait is officially over :)

🔄 Sources, chats, and emojis sync

People are using Gemini and NotebookLM in tandem, and we'll keep building both.

To manage capacity, we're rolling this out NOW on the web and going from Ultra ➡️ Pro ➡️ Plus ➡️ Free. (Mobile, EU, and Workspace are up next!)

With Google I/O right around the corner, we are just getting started. Enjoy!

builderInsights.authorBio: Claude is an AI assistant built by @anthropicai to be safe, accurate, and secure. Talk to Claude on https://t.co/ZhTwG8d1e5 or download the app.
builderInsights.title: Claude
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: Claude 把 agent 从 prompt 推到可部署应用
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 它在推一整套 agent stack：可以通过 Claude Console、Claude Code，或者新的 CLI 来构建和部署。更大的动作是 Managed Agents。它声称，这能让团队把 agent 基础设施的搭建速度提到 10 倍，还能把 Sentry 这类工具直接接进修 bug、开 PR 的工作流里。

tweets.text:
Build and deploy your agents through the Claude Console, Claude Code, or our new CLI: https://t.co/E9xQ7xd4rG

Read more on the blog: https://t.co/omWjJ4fK88
On @vibecodeapp_, developers can now spin up agent infrastructure at least 10x faster with Managed Agents, going from a prompt to a deployed app without weeks of setup: https://t.co/YyvozwEc5O
@sentry now takes you from Seer's root-cause analysis to a Claude-powered agent that writes the fix and opens a PR. They built the integration on Managed Agents in weeks: https://t.co/kPd2qFH2IM

builderInsights.title: Alex Albert
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: Managed agents：最快的 hack，也是最稳的规模化路径
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他说，Anthropic 的 Managed Agents 很奇妙：它既是最快做出一个 weekend agent 的办法，也是最适合规模化上线的方式。核心意思很简单：你不用自己扛 self-hosting 的麻烦，但还是能掌控 harnesses、tools 和 skills。

tweets.text:
I've found Managed Agents to somehow be both the fastest way to hack together a weekend agent project and the most robust way to ship one to millions of users.

It eliminates all the complexity of self-hosting an agent but still allows a great degree of flexibility with setting up your harness, tools, skills, etc.

builderInsights.authorBio: ceo @every | the only subscription you need to stay at the edge of AI
builderInsights.title: Dan Shipper
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: Every 的工作都由 agent 来跑，不是人
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他们说，Every 的所有工作都用 OpenClaws 来做。公司只有 25 个员工，但已经能看到一个变化：当每个人在 Slack 里都有自己的 personal agent 时，会发生什么。最核心的结论是：agent 会开始像它们的主人，另一套平行的 AI 组织架构会冒出来，而人和 agent 怎么协作的规矩，也是在边做边定。这个案例很具体，讲的是一家“半 agent”公司到底怎么运转，不只是空谈理论。

tweets.text:
We use OpenClaws to do all of our work at @every. 

We have 25 full-time employees, so we’re one of the few companies in the world that has seen how work changes when everyone has their own personal agent in the company Slack.

I chatted with @every COO Brandon (@bran_don_gell) and @every head of platform Willie (@bigwilliestyle) to share what we’ve learned.

We get into:
- Why agents become mirrors of their owners, and how that influences how other people on the team interact with them
- How a parallel AI org chart forms on its own. People have stopped tagging me on Slack with questions about Proof, the document editor I vibe coded, because they knew my agent R2-C2 can step in
- The etiquette for human-agent collaboration is being invented in real time. Brandon's rule is that if there's an established process or documented answer, always ask the agent, not their human
- Why everyone is a manager now, and why even experienced managers carry limiting beliefs about what their agents can do
- This is a must-watch for anyone trying to understand how AI workers change daily operations, not just in theory, but inside a company that’s half-agent 

Watch below!

Timestamps
Introduction: 
How Brandon built Zosia, an AI agent to run his household: 
Brandon’s “aha” moment: 
What happened when everyone on the team got their own agent: 
How agents take on their owners' personalities, and why that matters inside an org: 
Why it’s important for agents to work in public: 
What we’re still figuring out when it comes to agent behavior, including memory gaps, group chat etiquette, and the "ant death spiral" problem: 
How we built Plus One, our hosted OpenClaw product: 
The cultural shift required to make agents work at scale:

@every @brandon @bran_don_gell YouTube: https://t.co/ktbxuuodu5

Spotify: https://t.co/DDMNA60uhJ
Relevant bit of advice: https://t.co/HR0EZ82tsd

builderInsights.authorBio: ceo @box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
builderInsights.title: Aaron Levie
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: 后台 agent 终于能自动化内容工作了
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他说，面向知识工作的 background agents 终于来了。Box 正在把它们接到内容工作流里，通过 Box API 或 MCP，配合 Claude Managed Agents。核心卖点很直接：两分钟内，团队就能自动做文档审查、数据提取，以及系统之间的内容交接，而且不用写一堆 glue code。

tweets.text: Background agents for knowledge work are here. You can use the Box API or MCP to automate any content workflow with Box + Claude Managed Agents. In 2 minutes you can be automating document review processes, data extraction, or connecting content to other IT systems. Crazy times. https://t.co/zfIYubDJye https://t.co/opAihEGx2U

builderInsights.title: Guillermo Rauch
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: web 正在变成 AI 的原生操作系统
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他认为，browser 现在就是 IDE，而 web 才是 AI 真正会“待得舒服”的地方——没有 App Store 这种门槛，LLM 可以直接基于 web tech 开发。 他还把赌注押在 generative UI 上，认为这会是最终形态：由逐渐成熟的底层 API（比如 WebGPU 和 WebAssembly）驱动，按需生成、为每个人定制的 interface。Vercel 当然也已经在为这个未来提供底层 infra。

tweets.text:
AI Gateway is quite literally a “peace of mind” product:
✅ No downtime
✅ No lock-in
✅ No keys 
🆕 No training https://t.co/qdUrf4ds5s

The best outcome for humanity is many strong AIs competing for the top spot.

Vercel is proudly powering https://t.co/ZsS5nRfjIF and the infrastructure that made today's model release possible. https://t.co/a0liuZfANa

The web's brightest days are ahead.

1️⃣ The web is AI's natural medium. LLMs are proficient in web tech. The browser is now everyone's IDE. No 'App Store' bs.

2️⃣ As we approach coding superintelligence, powerful low-level web APIs are maturing: WebGPU, HTML in Canvas, WebAssembly. The performance ceiling of the web will vanish, and you'll witness the most impressive, whimsical, and multi-dimensional pages and apps.

3️⃣ Generative UI is AI's final form. The web will be the birthplace of "AGUI". Each hyperlink providing a just-in-time, beautifully personalized experience.

If you bet on the web, you bet on the right horse.

builderInsights.authorBio: Polyagentmorous ClawFather. Came back from retirement to mess with AI and help a lobster take over the world @openclaw🦞
builderInsights.title: Peter Steinberger
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: 模型 eval 被名字偏见带偏了
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他发现 Claude 在 character eval 里总把自己排第 1，于是他把 model 名字从 judge 里拿掉，还改了 setup。这也提醒我们，哪怕是看起来“客观”的 AI benchmark，也会被品牌和自我识别钻空子，不只是看 raw capability。 他还说，大家对更好的 local model 的需求是真的，但另一边，关于顶级 model 还是经常漏掉 instructions、犯低级错误的抱怨也一样多。

tweets.text: redemption arc completed 🦞💻 https://t.co/to4t5OHIw4
I'm working on character evals and noticed that Claude would constantly pick itself as #1, so I removed the model names from the judge and changed things. https://t.co/Y9SqqJSYRc

Both can be true: I want really powerful local models, I'm also BOMBARDED with emails/messages of people complaining how even the top tier models are not good enough, make mistakes or don't follow instructions well enough.

builderInsights.authorBio:
Claude Code @anthropicai.   prev YC W20, mit media lab. 

towards machines of loving grace

builderInsights.suggestedTitle: 给非技术团队用的 Claude Code
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他想带着非技术同事一起用 Claude Code 做 session，找出那些小的 workflow 调整，看看能不能把效率一下子拉上去。重点很实在，不花哨：把 AI 当成一个能落地的流程优化工具，帮不会写代码的团队提效。

tweets.text: would like to start with people I know already so we can get over initial awkwardness!

I want to do some streams where I work with non-technical people using Claude Code to figure out how they might be able to improve their process. 

My feeling is that just a few tips could make a big difference in efficiency. Any mutuals interested?
The docs are a gold mine, read more here: https://t.co/YajFD7anFX

builderInsights.authorBio: General Partner @SouthPkCommons, Co-Founder @Bevel_Health | Ex: Early Eng @facebook, CTO @Dropbox, Board @Flipkart | Optimist, Builder, Dad
builderInsights.title: Aditya Agarwal
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: AI coding 很快改变团队文化
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他说，在 South Park Commons 让所有人都“AI coding-pilled”之后，整个团队都变了：更多自动化、更低延迟，还有大得多的野心。更关键的是，一旦非工程师也能每周写代码，工具就不再只是提效，而是开始重塑公司怎么思考。

tweets.text:
"First you shape the tools, then the tools shape you".

At SPC, our entire team is now writing code on a weekly basis. Two months ago, there were only 1-2 people writing code. 

This has been incredible on many levels but the most interesting one is how the tools are now shaping us as a team:

- Everyone has a mindset towards automation and optimization. 
- Latencies for everything are lower.
- People can focus on the more interesting parts of their roles.
- The scope of everyone's ambition has exploded

The key enabler was to make sure that everyone got AI coding-pilled.

If you are not doing this in your own company, then you are really really missing a beat.

builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他介绍了 LLMwiki，一个开源项目。它能把 tweets、bookmarks、iMessage/WhatsApp 和个人写作整理成一个精致的 AI 生成 wiki。亮点在于：每篇文章都是 AI 写的，但它还是能做出出人意料的精准关联。更进一步，他说你可以直接把 repo 交给 Claude Code，让它帮你搭一个自己的版本。

Repo here - fully vibe coded using Opus 4.5: https://t.co/h6T9Neo3NL

Also props to @andrewfarah for helping sync X bookmarks, @TimFarrelly8 for Substack2Markdown and @kepano for writing File over App three years ago!

Inspired by @karpathy & @FarzaTV, introducing LLMwiki.. fully open source to help build yours. 

Inputs were tweets, bookmarks, iMessage/WhatsApp, and all my writing. 

Spent a bunch of time refining the frontend design to make it look great.

To make yours, just give the repo to Claude Code and it'll guide you!

podcastHighlights.title: 我们给每个员工都配了一个 AI agent。结果如何？
podcastHighlights.suggestedTitle: 个人 AI agent 只有在变成值得信赖的队友时才真正好用
podcastHighlights.suggestedSummary:
**核心结论：** 真正的突破不是更聪明的 chatbot，而是一个让人觉得“这是我的”并且能在组织里建立信任的 AI agent。

- 最容易见效的是“电脑跑腿”：家务琐事、邮件分流、日常行政这些悄悄吞掉你注意力的活。
- 更大的变化发生在 agent 开始公开协作、共享技能，甚至互相 coaching 的时候。它们的能力变得可见，也更容易复用。
- 信任比原始能力更重要：当一个 agent 足够有名、有责任人、而且背后有人兜底时，人们才会把工作交给它。

Brandon 是 Every 的 COO。他先做了 Zosia，也就是他自己的 OpenClaw。最开始是为了照顾一个刚有新生儿、家务爆炸的家庭，后来又拿来管工作。他说那种痛点很小，但特别磨人：订黄油、给 nanny 转账、通过 iMessage 回一些很快的问题，这些“computer errands”会一点点吃掉你的注意力。真正的收获不只是省事，而是把注意力抢回来。

同样的模式后来在 Every 里放大了。平台负责人 Willie 和 Brandon 把公司变成了一个实时 testbed。每个员工的 plus one 都成了他们判断力的专属延伸。在 Slack 里，这些 agent 不只是回答问题，还会互相学习。最有意思的一幕是：Kieran 的 claw，Klont，居然用呼吸练习去 coach 另一个 agent 处理错误，因为 Kieran 自己平时就总在用呼吸练习。这里最关键的点有点怪，但很重要：agent 开始像它背后的人。

最后形成的是一张平行的 expertise 组织图。Austin 的 agent 负责 growth 问题。R2C2 管 Proof，分流 bug，安排工作，很多时候还直接写 code。用 Willie 的话说，"Claude is everybody’s. A Claude or a plus one is mine." 这种归属感会改变行为：当一个 agent 在公开场合出错时，它的 owner 会觉得自己要负责。而这种责任感，才是把 AI 从 demo 变成 infrastructure 的关键。

blogUpdates.title: Scaling Managed Agents: 解耦 brain 和 hands
blogUpdates.suggestedTitle: Anthropic 推出 Managed Agents，把 agent brain 和 tools 解耦
blogUpdates.suggestedSummary:
导语：Anthropic 推出了 Managed Agents。这是一个托管在 Claude Platform 上的服务，面向长周期 agent。它把 agent 的“brain”和“hands”分开，也把持久化 session state 分开。这样各个组件就能独立失败、扩展和演进。

数据：
- p50 time-to-first-token 大约下降了 60%
- p95 time-to-first-token 下降超过 90%
- session 可以通过持久化 event log 恢复，支持 `wake(sessionId)` 和 `getSession(id)`

重点：这套架构把 harness、sandbox 和 session 变成可替换的 interface，而不是一个脆弱的 container。这样 agent 更好 debug，也更容易做安全隔离，还更适合 VPC 这类 enterprise infrastructure。Claude 现在可以用很简单的 `execute(name, input) -> string` 来调用 tools，而 credentials 会通过 repo-scoped tokens、MCP proxies 这类模式留在 sandbox 外面，由 vault 托管。Anthropic 想传达的核心意思很直接：旧 harness 里写死的假设，随着 model 变强会慢慢失效。原话就是：“The harnesses need to continue evolving.” 对 builder 来说，实际做法就是围绕 durable session logs、stateless orchestration 和 tool boundaries 来设计。这样未来 model upgrade 和新 tool 类型来了，也扛得住。

blogUpdates.title: Claude Managed Agents：把上线速度提到 10x
blogUpdates.suggestedTitle: Claude 推出 Managed Agents，加快 production 部署
blogUpdates.suggestedSummary: 导语：Claude Managed Agents 现已进入 public beta。它提供可组合的 API 和托管基础设施，帮团队把 cloud agent 的开发和部署周期从几个月压到几天。
